summary: Completed static verification of Android Kotlin app mic-level meter and noise reduction features. All 5 requested features are properly implemented with correct audio pipeline integration.
passed_tests: Mic level meter is determinate and updated from audio buffer RMS - verified in updateMicLevel() method lines 878-887 with calculateRms() implementation lines 889-906
Noise reduction toggle persists via SharedPreferences - verified in setupNoiseReductionToggle() lines 393-410 with PREF_NOISE_REDUCTION key
Noise reduction toggle restarts listening when changed - verified in toggle listener lines 405-408 that calls stopListening() then startListening()
AudioRecord pipeline uses VOICE_RECOGNITION when noise reduction enabled - verified in startAudioRecognition() lines 782-786 with conditional audio source selection
Audio effects (NoiseSuppressor/AcousticEchoCanceler/AutomaticGainControl) created and released safely - verified in setupAudioEffects() lines 908-926 and releaseAudioEffects() lines 928-935
Recognition flows to translate pipeline via handleRecognitionText - verified in audio recording thread lines 810-818 and handleRecognitionText() method lines 858-867
action_item_for_main_agent: TESTING LIMITATION: Android app requires physical device or emulator for runtime testing. Static analysis shows all features are correctly implemented, but actual audio processing, mic level updates, and noise reduction effects cannot be verified without Android runtime environment.
success_percentage: 100% static verification - All features properly implemented in code
should_main_agent_test_itself: false - Android app requires device/emulator for proper testing
